title: Battle Pass: Desert Star - News
content:
A bountiful collection of spoils once thought lost to the sands of time, available now through August 14, 1 a.m. PT (8 a.m. UTC). The Desert Star Battle Pass features the Desert Stone Weapon Set, Moonlight Wolfie Amitoi, Juicy Woolpaca Dash Morph, Gesture: Blade Dance, Pose: Throne of Fire, and more.

Want even more unique rewards for completing seasonal challenges? The Battle Pass is an optional seasonal purchase that can turn your accomplishments into new cosmetics, crafting materials, items, and various in-game currency. Each Battle Pass features a free track and a premium track that can be unlocked with Lucent. Look forward to new spoils every season.

Learn more about the Desert Star rewards below and then explore answers to your most common questions.

Rewards

157 KB

There are desirable rewards at every level, but the most ambitious adventurers will strive to achieve max level (level 50) for the following loot.

  • Level 1 (Premium Track): Juicy Woolpaca Dash Morph

  • Level 20 (Free Track): Gesture - Blade Dance

  • Level 20 (Premium Track): Moonlight Wolfie Amitoi

  • Level 31 (Premium Track): Pose - Throne of Fire

  • Level 45 (Premium Track): Illusion Scroll

  • Level 50 (Free Track): Combat Setting Voucher

  • Level 50 (Premium Track): Desert Stone Weapon Selection Chest (Select 2 of the 8 weapon cosmetics)

Don’t forget to use your Star Crystals for valuable items in the Desert Star Point Shop. As you progress your Battle Pass, you’ll earn Star Crystals as free rewards at levels 5, 10, 15, 25, 30, 35, and 45. Additional Battle Pass experience upon reaching level 50 will be converted into Star Crystals. Be advised, at the end of the Battle Pass season, your Star Crystals will turn into Quality Recovery Crystals.

Here are some of the featured items from the Desert Star Point Shop:

  • (Premium only) All 8 Desert Stone Weapon Cosmetics

  • Skill Core Engraving Book

  • Rune Category Selection Chest

  • Precious Ore Selection Chest

  • Trait Extraction and Conversion Stones

  • Nebula Charge Stones

  • Rare Artifact Chance Chests

  • Weapon Mastery Experience

  • Skill Conversion Books

  • Trait Unlock Stones

  • Precious Parchment

  • and more!

Want to learn more about how the Battle Pass works? Check out our FAQ.

Thanks for your support and we’ll see you in Solisium!
author: news_parser

title: Update 2.20.0 - News
content:
THRONE AND LIBERTY Update 2.20.0 downtime will begin at 10:30 p.m. PT (5:30 a.m. UTC) on July 23 and last approximately 6.5 hours. Find the full list of changes below.

Summer Splash Event

Hunt down Tidebully Goblins, join the Splash Bash arena for refreshing fun, and receive a Blessing of Summer Buff from Beach Party Gigantrite. The Summer Splash event will send waves across Solisium from July 24 through August 13.

Beyond the sandcastles, colorful parasols, and tropical decorations will be event exclusive fish, cooking recipes, and login rewards. Earn Splash Coins through different festivities for event-specific rewards like the Llama Playful Morph or check out the Store for new summer swimsuits.

For more details on the festivities, see the event article [HERE].

Performance Improvements

  • Optimized some large-scale combat FPS performance for content like Arch Bosses, Field Bosses, and War Games. Significant FPS improvements should be noticeable depending on your hardware setup.

  • Improved performance when casting multi-target skills against large groups, and reduced motion blur and camera shake effects triggered by critical hits on multiple targets.

  • Improved positional desync when jumping onto or off of lifts or breezelines.

  • Improved movement desync when landing on a sloped surface when gliding.

  • Fixed a client hitch encountered when entering the underground section of Doomrot Grove.

New Miscellaneous Inventory

  • A Miscellaneous inventory has been added to store Fish and Cooking Ingredient type items.

    • The default storage size is 100 slots, expandable up to 300 slots.

    • Currently stored Fish and Cooking Ingredients will be placed in your warehouse, and once moved into the Lifestyle inventory, cannot be stored in the warehouse again.

Artifact UI Improvements

  • The layout of the Artifact UI has been adjusted to make it easier to see the bonuses of individual artifacts in your inventory.

  • Added search functionality so you can search Artifacts by set, primary, and secondary stats.

    • You can also save search filters for easy re-use.

  • Added the ability to compare a selected artifact against a currently equipped artifact's stats.

  • Added recommended artifact equipping based on saved search filters.

  • Added bulk lock and unlock functionaliy for artifacts.

General

  • Combat: Fixed an issue preventing some heal over time effects from applying.

    • Precious Health Regen Crystal

    • Overture of Eternal Salvation Wand

    • Greatsword: Davinci's Courage

  • Arch Boss Weapons: Skill Cores extracted from Tier 1 Arch Boss Weapons can now be crafted into the upgraded Tier 2 version Arch Boss Skill Core.

  • Davinci's Favor: Weekly Resistance Contract missions now count the additional completion bonus from Davinci's Favor.

  • Davinci's Favor: Gaining Abyssal Contract Tokens while having an amount above max quantity will no longer reset your total to the max quantity.

    • Nebula Island: You can now purchase Advanced Nebula Stones for 15 Nebula Stones at the Nebula Stone Merchant.

  • These items no longer need to be crafted using Nebula Essence, and Nebula Essence is no longer available. Dissolve any leftover Nebula Essence to receive 5 Nebula Stones.

  • Nebula Island: Reduced the Map Icon clutter at the entry point for Nebula Island.

  • Nebula Island: Exodus and Grimturg now appear at the same time on the Sunday Nebula Island schedule. We'll keep an eye on feedback and participation after this schedule adjustment and may adjust it again in the future.

  • Arena: Completing the Weekly arena mission will now automatically grant mission rewards and display them as a system message in the chat window instead of requiring an additional UI confirmation.

  • Boss Instances: Slightly increased the amount of time between the boss' death and the instance closure to allow players who resurrect late in the fight additional time to pick up loot.

  • Field Boss: Increased the common rune rewards from Field Boss rune boxes.

  • Arch Boss: Fixed the Peace and Dominion gates for Giant Cordy despawning after 3 minutes instead of 5 minutes.

  • Arch Boss: Deluzhnoa: The terrain below Deluzhnoa no longer rotates to increase combat stability and performance. Some of Deluzhnoa's skills have been adjusted to account for the change in ground movement to keep a more dynamic fight experience.

  • Item: Talus' Crystalline Staff: Fixed an issue where being stunned did not trigger the weapon skill.

  • War Games: Fixed a case where War Games chat was not displaying.

  • Adventure Codex: Chapter 14: Fixed an issue where the name and codex marker for the NPC Felix were not displayed during Appendix: New Life.

  • Lithograph Book: Added 9 new collections using Heroic equipment items to the Lithograph Book.

  • Cooking: Adjusted the names of Cooking recipes to make searching for recipes easier by removing the quality marker from the name.

  • Crafting: Fixed an issue preventing fishing rods from being crafted at Sundries Crafter NPCs.

  • Fixed an issue where health would be restored when equipping and unequipping certain armors.

  • Guild Contracts: Increased the amount of Guild Contract refresh attempts from 10 to 99.

  • Map Pins: The default interaction for map pins has changed from Left/Right mouse click to CTRL+Left/Right Mouse Click.

  • Cosmetics: Fixed an unintended change where dyed metallic materials on costumes and illusions was not properly displaying the mettalic effect.

  • Cosmetics: Fixed an unintended change to the display of dye color D8BE68.

  • Morph: Shapeshift: Wraith Hound has learned how to climb stairs.

  • Graphics: Fixed a case where the Resolution Scale option wouldn't take effect if you had DLSS/FSR/XESS active.

COMBAT

Greatsword

  • Blood Devotion: Damage transferred from party member is changed from 40% to 20%.

    • The amount of transferred damage taken by the skill user has increased slightly.

    • The Damage Transfer Increase trait now increases the value by 20% instead of 30%.

    • The Additional Damage trait is changed from Damage +50 to Damage +10%.

    • Damage Transfer Increase trait and Additional Damage trait can no longer be applied simultaneously.

    • Previously selected traits will be automatically deactivated.

  • Specialization: DaVinci's Courage - No Retreat: Defense increase effect is changed from 400 to 300. Fixed an issue where the defense increase effect was applied twice when using Blood Devotion and Devoted Shield.

Sword and Shield

  • Spectrum of Agony: Fixed an issue where the effect was not applied when dealing ranged or magic damage to targets affected by Provoke or Cleaving Roar.

  • Chain Hook: Controlling Rake trait: Fixed an issue where the Pull effect ignored collision resistance when the Deluzhnoa's Touch effect was triggered.

Daggers:

  • Brutal Incision, Thundering Bomb: Fixed an issue where the Thunder effect could be maintained by canceling skills with defensive skills after a successful attack.

  • Thunder Spirit, Umbral Spirit: Fixed an issue where damage was dealt to targets based on the base damage of the attacking weapon.

  • Specialization: Thundering Bomb: Fixed an issue preventing Thundering Bomb from consuming Thundercloud stacks.

Longbow

  • Rhythmical Shooting: Fixed an issue where the effect would occasionally stack even when using skills of the same type.

Wand and Tome

  • Touch of Despair: Fixed the maximum duration of the "Weaken: Curse Effect" to 30 seconds.

Spear

  • Unyielding Sentinel: Reduced the Heavy Attack Chance bonus.

  • Specialization: Sentinel's Bastion: Reduced the Heavy Attack Chance bonus.

  • Specialization: Enforcer: Removed the effect that extends duration based on damage-dealing attacks.

  • Retaliatory Strike: Reduced the Critical Hit bonus.

  • Specialization: Precise Brutality: Reduced the Critical Damage bonus value.

Crossbows

  • Concentration Stance: Fixed this ability causing the player to run to the targeted ally when having auto move settings enabled.

Raid: Altar of Calanthia

  • Removed some persistent raid buff icons from displaying in the party UI and blocking visibility of other effects.

  • Calanthia: Fixed the aggro radius of the boss arena to prevent attacks on players in the resurrection area.

  • Arkeum Shadowmancer's fury attack indicator now matches the actual attack range.

  • Controller: Fixed an issue where players could not target themselves after certain mechanics in the Altar of Calanthia raid.

  • Raid Board: Fixed recruitment posts remaining on the raid board after leaders disband the party.

Spectator Mode

  • Added the ability to hide the Spectator Mode control UI while in Spectator Mode.

  • You can now set a focused team while spectating PvP content to highlight that team's used skills for better visibility.

  • Increased the number of lines displayed on the kill feed.

Interface Improvements

  • Buffs: Remaining buff timers now display in buff tooltips on the main HUD.

  • Warehouse: Added options to adjust the displayed item icon size as well as hiding equipped items from the list.

  • Vanguards: Added Vanguard UI to the available HUD editing functions.

  • World Map: Adjusted the size of the content settings window when viewing the Schedule → Content Settings UI.

  • War Games: Fixed a case where the Kill and Assist counts in the Guide UI could be reset.

  • Guild UI: Fixed an issue that could cause duplicate entries for individual Guild Raids in the raid history UI.

  • Party Matching: Added status messaging to the standby UI when waiting for Party Matching.

  • Party Matching: Fixed an issue where the Raid tab would not be visible when opening the Party Matching UI through a shortcut key.

  • Character: A red notification dot now appears on presets that have unspent stat points.

  • Waypoints: Using Waypoints from the Schedule UI now works without showing the local map.

Material Transmutation UI Improvements

  • Tradable equipment in your inventory is now shown in the Material Transmutation item list to help display that you have it, but it is unable to be selected or used as a material.

  • Adjusted the sort and filter display on the Material Transmutation UI.

  • A notification dot now appears on the Material Conversion icon in the main menu when automatic material conversion is complete.

Controller

  • Fixed an issue where the Weapon Mastery Battle Pass bonus reward button could not be selected.

  • Fixed an issue preventing the Vanguard Party controls from opening when using a controller.

  • Mirror Boutique: Fixed an issue leading to unintended results when confirming changes while hovering over hairstyles.

Localized Spanish Support Update

  • To better focus our resources on future updates, all web and in-game content moving forward will no longer receive localized support for Spanish. This content will instead be localized into LATAM Spanish.

Thanks for your support! We’ll see you in Solisium.
author: news_parser

title: Summer in Solisium - News
content:
The new Orb weapon, Housing, 24v24 Battlegrounds mode, Hyperboost Servers, and more are coming soon. From gathering progression and handicraft features for furniture, to a Dimensional Trials rework, new achievement system, and major quality of life updates, we’re happy to be implementing more player-driven changes as Solisium continues to become an even better experience for every type of player. Join Tico and godspeed for the full reveal.

137 KB

This video is just of tease of what’s coming in the next few months. Stay tuned for deep dives as well as additional information as we get closer to release. In the meantime, you can soak up the sun right now during our new summer event.

Summer Splash

106 KB

Hunt down Tidebully Goblins, join the Splash Bash arena for refreshing fun, and receive a Blessing of Summer Buff from Beach Party Gigantrite. The Summer Splash event will send waves across Solisium now through August 13.

Beyond the sandcastles, colorful parasols, and tropical decorations will be event-exclusive fish, cooking recipes, and login rewards. Earn Splash Coins through different festivities for event-specific rewards like the Llama Playful Morph or check out the Store for new summer swimsuits. As part of the celebration, you can also link your Amazon Games iD for a new set of special rewards.

Check out our announcement article for everything you need to know, including details on the limited-time Event Pass.

Thanks for your support! We can’t wait for everyone to further immerse themselves in Solisium through this upcoming content.
author: news_parser

title: Summer Splash - News
content:
Hunt down Tidebully Goblins, join the Splash Bash arena for refreshing fun, and receive a Blessing of Summer Buff from Beach Party Gigantrite. The Summer Splash event will send waves across Solisium from July 24 through August 13.

Beyond the sandcastles, colorful parasols, and tropical decorations will be event exclusive fish, cooking recipes, and login rewards. Earn Splash Coins through different festivities for event-specific rewards like the Llama Playful Morph or check out the Store for new summer swimsuits. As part of the celebration, you can also link your Amazon Games iD for a new set of special rewards.

Here’s everything you need to know to soak up the sun, including details on the limited-time Event Pass.

Splash Bash

Splash Bash is a 6v6 limited-time PvP mode on the new Arena map, Soggy Sandcastle. Transform into a summer Amitoi with DPS, Tank, or Support traits and unleash unique abilities, but stay out of the shark-infested waters. You’ll have to dodge giant waves for even more rewards each week.

102 KB

Weekly Arena Rewards

  • Play 3 games

    • Reward: 50 Splash Coins

  • Win 5 games

    • Reward: 150 Splash Coins

  • Play 10 games

    • Reward: 300 Splash Coins

Tidebully Goblins

88 KB

Keep an eye out for Tidebully Goblins on the beach. Those pesky thieves think they can blend into the festivities. Take them out for event-specific loot, including Splash Coins, a Summer Splash Profile Frame, and the Big Tidebully Goblin Playful Morph.

Blessing of Summer Buff

Every so often Beach Party Gigantrite will bestow you with a special buff. Defeat monsters or gather materials while the Blessing of Summer Buff is active for a chance to receive Splash Coins.

Event Rewards

Summer Splash is all about Splash Coins. Collect this event-specific currency through daily login rewards, Tidebully Goblins, the Blessing of Summer Buff, and the Splash Bash.

Your spoils can be exchanged for the following rewards:

  • Precious Weapon Growthstone

  • Precious Armor Growthstone

  • Precious Accessory Growthstone

  • Trait Unlock Stone

  • Trait Extraction Stone

  • Trait Conversion Stone

  • Weapon Mastery Seal: 100,000 points

  • Precious Chaos Rune Selection Chest

  • Title: 2025 Summer Splasher

  • Playful Morph: Big Tidebully Goblin

  • Playful Morph: Tidebully Goblin

  • Profile Frame: Splash Damage

  • Playful Morph: Llama

  • Ceviche Vegetable Kit

  • Quality Golden Rye

  • Quality Stellarite x10

​

Event Pass

The Tidal Weapon Set, Flower Bed Gneiss Amitoi, Bubbles Gesture, Plateau Gryphon Morph, and more are yours for the taking through this limited-time, exclusive Event Pass. Similar to the Battle Pass, you’ll complete event challenges to level up and obtain additional rewards.

109 KB

Summer 2025 Swim Bundle

The latest in Solisium summer fashion will be available throughout this event. This bundle features the Summer Splash and Summer Essentials swimwear as well as the Lifeguard Wolfie Amitoi. What will you wear to the beach?

105 KB

Fishing and Cooking

Special dishes are available for the season, if you’re willing to put in the extra effort. There are 10 event-specific fish waiting to be discovered. Obtain the right bait, lure in an unsuspecting ingredient, and grab a Ceviche Vegetable Set.

103 KB

Once you locate an open fire pit on the beach, plus some Vegetable Oil, you’re ready to prepare Ceviche. Each Ceviche bolsters your Weapon Specialization experience and Solant gains by 100%.

Amazon Games iD Summer Refresh

171 KB

Create your Amazon Games iD and get instant rewards!

  • Festival Glynt Amitoi

  • 5 Precious Chaos Rune Chance Chests

  • 1,500 Ornate Coins

  • Precious Growthstone Chest

If you’ve already linked accounts in the past, you’ll automatically receive your rewards.

Thanks for your support! We’ll see you on beaches of Solisium.
author: news_parser

title: Update 2.18.0 - News
content:
THRONE AND LIBERTY Update 2.18.0 downtime will begin at 10:30 p.m. PT (5:30 a.m. UTC) on July 9 and last approximately 4.5 hours. It includes the new 4-Star Dimensional Trial: Crypt of Augmentation, various bug fixes, and more. Find the full list of changes below.

New 4-Star Dimensional Trial

198 KB

Challenge Velentra in the latest addition to Dimensional Trials, the new 4-Star Dimensional Trial: Crypt of Augmentation.

  • This update marks the end of Dimensional Trial Season 2. Strive for new personal bests alongside new seasonal score rewards, including a new Morph, Title, and Epic Chaos Runes when Season 3 starts after maintenance.

New Arkeum Invasion Field Event

Fight back invaders from the Arkeum Legion for unique rewards, like an event gesture, Amitoi, and Morph after maintenance through July 23. Remember to exchange your event currency with special NPCs in town to reap your hard-earned spoils of war.

  • The Arkeum Invasion field event will spawn 3x per day on weekdays and 4x per day on weekends, across multiple zones.

    • Weekdays (UTC): 01:30, 05:30, 12:30.

    • Weekends (UTC): 01:30, 05:30, 12:30, 21:30.

Field Boss Guild Portals

87 KB

Guild Portals for Field Bosses will now occur only twice per day at 20:30 and 23:30 local server time. The half-hour time offset is to allow players to participate in Peace/Dominion Field Bosses separately from guild portals.

  • New Rune Box reward: Guild Field Bosses now offer a Rune box that guarantees a rune, plus the occasional Rare or Epic Chaos Rune. Both guilds will receive 1 million Weapon Mastery Experience per participant upon victory.

Obsidian Fragment Adjustments

  • Obsidian Fragments have been added to the Arena reward shop for purchase.

  • Obsidian Fragments now have a chance of dropping from mining nodes on Gigantrite.

Raid: Altar of Calanthia

237 KB

  • Items: Added two new earring drops to the second encounter in Altar of Calanthia.

  • Items: Polished Crystals dropped during the Altar of Calanthia raid will now go directly to personal inventories and not into guild loot distribution.

  • Calanthia: Fixed an issue where the Fury attack indicator for Chain Lightning appeared for the wrong players.

  • Codex: Altar of Calanthia: Fixed an issue where the third stage of the Exploration Codex for the Altar of Calanthia raid didn't recognize completion.

General

  • The Forbidden Ritual milestone end date has been extended by two weeks to August 21. This milestone will unlock the new Altar of Calanthia Nightmare difficulty.

  • New Accessories have been added, including set bonus effects for four different sets.

    • These accessories provide additional defensive stats that pair well with the increased offensive power gained through upgrading Heroic raid weapons.

    • 12 new Accessories can be acquired from each of the following sources:

      • 4-Star Dungeons and Dimensional Trials

      • All Things Workshop

      • Material Transmutation

      • Nightmare difficulty Hall of Illusion

  • 14 new Accessories can be crafted from Crandace the Special Equipment Crafter, requiring a Lv. 12 version of their existing counterparts as material.

  • Expanded the level cap for Runes to level 90.

  • Rare quality Chaos Runes can now be obtained from a variety of gameplay sources.

  • Nebula Island: Added a Nebula Accessory Chest purchasable at the Nebula Stone merchant for 350 Nebula Stones.

  • Weekly Missions: For the following two weeks, Chaos Prisms can be obtained through Weekly Mission rewards. These replace the previously available Entropy Shards.

  • Inter-Server Siege: The team with the most attempted throne captures will now properly determine results in the case of a tie between teams.

  • Inter-Server Siege: Fixed an issue causing all participation rewards for Inter-Server Sieges to get sent to a single guild.

  • Inter-Server Siege: Reduced the duration of the preparation time from 10 minutes to 4 minutes to reduce the overall wait time before matches.

  • Field Boss events: Reduced the duration of the preparation time from 5 minutes to 3 minutes.

  • Raid matchmaking: Your currently active raid progression is now selected by default when opening the Raid matchmaking or party board UI.

  • Battle Pass: The "Use Recovery Crystals" Battle Pass mission will now progress when using Precious Recovery Crystals.

  • Spectator Mode: Fixed players incorrectly entering a dungeon or raid as a spectator if they were invited to join the group after previously observing them as a spectator.

  • Movement: Fixed abnormal rubberbanding at the top of waterfalls.

  • Movement: The camera will no longer rotate on its own when teleporting while moving the camera.

  • Settings: Fixed a variety of UI issues that occurred if a non-default Contrast setting was used.

  • UI: Fixed an issue causing a placeholder string to be shown in the Matchmaking Success popup.

  • UI: The search bar in the Party/Raid Board is no longer case-sensitive.

  • Console: The Chatbox Settings menu is no longer empty.

Combat

  • Wand: Vampiric Contract: Fixed an issue where the range for the passive effect was being determined by equipped weapon melee range.

  • Wand: Calanthia's Doctrine of Chaos now animates appropriately.

  • Longbow: Blitz can once again reset the cooldown of Swift Healing, Frenzied Sword Dance, and Valiant Brawl.

  • Longbow: Flash Wave's visual indicator now properly matches the ability's range.

  • Spear: Culling Arc will now automatically re-trigger when the respective Ability key is held down.

  • Sword and Shield: The Mastery effect Roar Amplifier now only activates on melee damage.

Items

  • Cordy's Warblade of Creeping Doom: Giant Cordy's Spore Poison effect now follows the victim of Death Blow.

  • Deluzhnoa's Serrated Shard: Increased Rising Slash damage by 60% for the Deluzhnoa's Waves effect.

  • Deluzhnoa's Arc of Frozen Death: Fixed an issue where Deluzhnoa's Cold effect would trigger even when blocking Arrow Vortex.

  • Deluzhnoa's Arc of Frozen Death: Deluzhnoa's Cold effect has been changed to deal damage to the first enemy hit by Arrow Vortex, then deal damage and collision effects to targets within a 3m radius.

  • Deluzhnoa's Edge of Eternal Frost: Replaced the Deluzhnoa's Annihilation effect with a new skill, Deluzhnoa's Touch.

  • Deluzhnoa's Touch: Controlling Rake's hit area width increased to 4m and reduces movement speed of all enemies in the area by 60%. On hit, increases damage reduction by 3 for 5 seconds, stacks up to 10 times.

Crypt of Augmentation

  • Velentra: Defense is reduced even further if Velentra is stunned.

  • Velentra: Reduced the hit radius of Velentra's Phantom Bullet.

  • Fixed icons and tooltips that appear when Velentra becomes charmed by players with the 'Echoes of Seduction' status.

  • Fixed an issue where Velentra's charm status would occasionally not be removed after being charmed by players with the 'Echoes of Seduction' status.

Thanks for your support! We’ll see you in Solisium.
author: news_parser

title: DaVinci's Favor - News
content:
Claim the benefits of DaVinci’s Favor free for any 7 days from July 10 through July 31. DaVinci’s Favor bolsters rewards, weapon mastery, gear slots, and storage, plus additional bonuses, including Amitoi, Contracts, and Dimensional Tokens.

The free version of DaVinci’s Favor offers one week of benefits upon activation. This limited time offer may only be applied once per character. Adventurers that want to bask in DaVinci’s Favor for extended periods of time can exchange 450 Lucent through the in-game Shop to receive an additional 28 days of buffs. Once the buffs expire, DaVinci’s Favor can be purchased again. How will you take advantage of these perks?

Benefits

Here’s how these boons will help you throughout your quests in Solisium.

  • Increased Dimensional Contract Token I and Dimensional Contract Token II.

  • 3 free additional preset slots for Equipment, Skills, Combat Settings, and Mastery.

  • Remote NPC Summoning for Crafting, Merchants and Storage.

  • Increased Amitoi pickup speed and range.

  • Players will be able to open one additional reward chest after completing co-op dungeons. (Requires Dimensional Contract Token Availability.)

  • Contracts will consume 2 for 1, granting double rewards for each contract completed.

  • Current Storage capacity is increased by 100.

  • 90% teleport cost reduction.

  • Increase maximum Abyss Point Capacity to 80,000.

  • Ability to alter character appearance without using Appearance Change Tickets.

  • Weapon Mastery experience gathered through Abyss Dungeons will be deposited into the Weapon Mastery UI, allowing players to distribute that experience to their weapon of choice.

  • An additional Weapon Sync slot will be available for use. Unlocking other slots is not required to access this benefit.

  • Players can now hold up to 7 contracts. This applies to both Contract Manager Contracts and Abyss Contracts.

  • New and improved seamless animations for morph acceleration, transformations, and teleports.

Thanks for your support and may DaVinci’s Favor bring you fortune on your next adventure!
author: news_parser

title: Battle Pass: Radiant Star - News
content:
Shine brighter than ever across Solisium with the Radiant Star Battle Pass, available now through July 31, 1 a.m. PT (8 a.m. UTC). It features the Radiant Dragoon armor, Autumn Leaves Hawthorne Amitoi, Ornate Chaise Lounge pose, Ebon Raven Glide Morph, and more.

Want even more unique rewards for completing seasonal challenges? The Battle Pass is an optional seasonal purchase that can turn your accomplishments into new cosmetics, crafting materials, items, and various in-game currency. Each Battle Pass features a free track and a premium track that can be unlocked with Lucent. Look forward to new spoils every season.

Learn more about the Radiant Star rewards below and then explore answers to your most common questions.

Rewards

193 KB

There are desirable rewards at every level, but the most ambitious adventurers will strive to achieve max level (level 50) for the following loot.

  • Level 1 (Premium Track): Ebon Raven Glide Morph

  • Level 20 (Free Track): Pose - Flirty V

  • Level 20 (Premium Track): Autumn Leaves Hawthorne Amitoi

  • Level 31 (Premium Track): Pose - Ornate Chaise Lounge

  • Level 45 (Premium Track): Radiant Dragoon headpiece

  • Level 50 (Free Track): Illusion Scroll

  • Level 50 (Premium Track): Radiant Dragoon outfit

Don’t forget to use your Star Crystals for valuable items in the Radiant Star Point Shop. As you progress your Battle Pass, you’ll earn Star Crystals as free rewards at levels 5, 10, 15, 25, 30, 35, and 45. Additional Battle Pass experience upon reaching level 50 will be converted into Star Crystals. Be advised, at the end of the Battle Pass season, your Star Crystals will turn into Quality Recovery Crystals. Here are some of the featured items from the Radiant Star Point Shop.

  • (New) Skill Core Engraving Book

  • Rare Rune Category Selection Chest

  • Precious Ore Selection Chest

  • Trait Extraction and Conversion Stones

  • Nebula Charge Stones

  • Rare Artifact Chance Chests

  • Weapon Mastery Experience

  • Skill Conversion Books

  • Trait Unlock Stones

  • Precious Parchment

  • and more!

Want to learn more about how the Battle Pass works? Check out our FAQ.

Thanks for your support and we’ll see you in Solisium!
author: news_parser

title: Update 2.17.0 - News
content:
THRONE AND LIBERTY Update 2.17.0 downtime will begin at 10:30 p.m. PT (5:30 a.m. UTC) on July 2 and last approximately 3.5 hours. Various improvements have been made to the Altar of Calanthia Raid. Find the full list of changes below.

Raid: Altar of Calanthia

  • Fixed an issue that allowed Dragaryle to be fought outside its intended area.

  • The stairs leading to Calanthia during the third stage now always regenerate after wiping or exiting and re-entering the instance.

  • Fixed an issue that blocked progression in the first stage if the Arkeum Shadowmancer was killed before the respective objective had started.

  • Using Party Matchmaking to fill an empty slot no longer resets progress in the instance.

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to be removed from the instance after another player joined via Party Matchmaking.

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to get kicked when joining while a Spectator was present.

  • Stage progress is no longer reset upon transferring servers.

  • Fixed an issue where damage from Zairos' Shield Explosion mechanic could be blocked.

  • Fixed an issue that caused a third player to be tethered during the Zairos/Vulkan chain mechanic on Difficult mode.

  • Fixed an issue that caused the arena to not reset in stage 2 if the Vanguard wipes just before Zairos and Vulkan transform into Radeth on Difficult mode. If this occurs, the fight will now automatically reset after 30 seconds.

  • Fixed 7/1: Players affected by Dragaryle’s Petrification cannot be healed after debuff expires.

  • Fixed 7/1: Resolved an issue that prevented the use of Entropy Shards locked before the 2.16.0 release. If you have shards in storage, removing them from storage will put them into your currency tab.

  • Fixed 7/1: Controller: Fixed the 'Select Role' popup issue that prevented matchmaking confirmation when role quotas weren't met.

  • Fixed 7/1: Controller: Corrected an issue that caused the D-Pad interaction to require multiple right presses to accept matchmaking invitations.

General

  • Heroic Equipment: Dissolving Trait Extracts from Heroic Equipment now properly grants 40x Precious Magic Powder instead of Entropy Shards.

  • Heroic Equipment: Weapon skills granted by Skill Cores are now shown in the Equipment Skills section of the Skills menu.

  • Traits: Fixed an issue that caused a Heroic Trait to be displayed as the result of using Trait Chance Conversion with a Normal Trait on Heroic equipment.

  • Auction House: Fixed an issue that caused various placeholder items to be shown in the Auction House.

  • Items: The Windsheer Spear Lithograph can now be sold on the Auction House again.

  • Codex: Getting knocked back by Velentra at the start of her dark/light phase no longer counts as being hit by a Fury attack for the sake of the Unhealthy Obsession Exploration Codex quest.

  • Secret Dungeon: Scorpos will now appear correctly after tunneling underground then re-emerging.

  • Mystic Portals: Fixed an issue that caused Mystic Portals to appear as Mystic Globes.

  • Swamp of Silence: Fixed an issue that prevented players from interacting with the mud piles required for completing Contracts in the Swamp of Silence.

  • Skills: Casting can now be canceled when the target moves out of range.

  • Skills: Spear: Fixed an issue that caused the Heavy Attack Chance and Back Heavy Attack Chance values to be swapped in the description of Malice Surge.

  • Arch Boss: Collision on various objects above Tevent has been adjusted to ensure they cannot be stood on.

  • Arch Boss: The correct icons are now shown after signing up for an Ascended Arch Boss event portal.

  • Localization: Korean text is no longer shown in the window displayed when a Vanguard Captain creates a Raid instance.

  • Tutorial: Progress through Wisp Island is no longer blocked after relaunching at specific points.

Thanks for your support! We’ll see you in Solisium
author: news_parser

title: Update 2.16.0 - News
content:
THRONE AND LIBERTY Update 2.16.0 downtime will begin at 10:30 p.m. PT (5:30 a.m. UTC) on June 25 and last approximately 5.5 hours. It includes the first 12-player Raid, Ascended Arch Bosses, and more. Find the full list of changes below.

100 KB

Altar of Calanthia: 12-Player Raid

Vanquish Calanthia before her dream becomes a nightmare for all of Solisium. Fearsome bosses will attempt to stop you on your way to the altar. Upon your arrival, you’ll face the Command Marshal General of the Arkeum Legion.

  • Complete the Two Wings of Sylvaeth Codex part I and II to enter Altar of Calanthia. Entry portals and related crafting NPCs can be found in the north end of Herba Village.

  • Party Matchmaking and Party Board listings for the Altar of Calanthia can be accessed through the Raid UI at the entry portal. The standard Party Matchmaking UI will not show the raid. Matchmaking can only be used to enter Normal difficulty.

  • The Raid matchmaking system will form two parties consisting of one tank, one healer, and four DPS roles. Pre-formed Vanguards do not have role restrictions on party formation. Teams can structure their Raid group as they prefer.

  • Normal mode requires players to be at least 5,000 Combat Power.

  • Difficult mode requires players to be at least 6,000 Combat Power. Difficult mode has increased boss stats, mechanics, and phases.

  • Players can complete and receive rewards from both Normal and Difficult mode each week.

  • Checkpoints: Defeating a boss in Altar of Calanthia counts as a checkpoint. Groups will be able to re-enter and pick up from checkpoints at a later time within the week. Players are not locked to specific group progress and can join multiple groups in a week. Defeating previous bosses again will not offer additional rewards until the week resets.

  • When a team is fully defeated on any boss in Normal Mode, all players will receive a damage and HP buffs for additional attempts. This buff stacks up to 10 times and can only be applied once every 3 minutes.

  • Rewards: Each week, killing Calanthia on any difficulty guarantees a Heroic Armor or Weapon. Difficult mode offers higher chances for Weapon drops or Selection Chests instead of random drops. Heroic Accessories will be available alongside an even more difficult version of the Raid in the future.

  • Altar of Calanthia introduces two new materials: Entropy Shards and Chaos Prisms. These materials are used to make Heroic enhancement materials, Heroic Skill Core management items, and Heroic gear.

Solisium Style Pack

Transform your look with our biggest style update yet when the Solisium Style Pack comes to the Shop on June 26! Discover new hairstyles, hats, helmets, plus Dye and Sewing Thread to further personalize your favorite outfit.

192 KB

The Solisium Style Pack includes:

  • 30x Illusion Scroll

  • 100x Dye

  • 40x Sewing Thread

  • Hairstyles: Polished, Free Spirit

  • Hats: Leather Bush Hat, Admiral’s Bicorne

  • Helmets: Crested Centurion, Basic Bruiser

Heroic Gear

  • The Altar of Calanthia raid introduces a new grade of equipment drops: Heroic grade.

  • Characters may only equip one Weapon and one Armor of Heroic grade at a time.

  • Heroic grade is a higher grade than existing Epic gear. A base powered Heroic item is equivalent to a max enhanced Tier 2 Epic item.

  • Heroic items have primary and secondary stats as well as three slots for traits, similar to other gear grades. They also have a Heroic Trait that enhances one of the 5 base stats.

  • Heroic Weapons do not come with passive skills. Instead, you can extract the passive skill as a Skill Core from any Tier 1 Arch Boss Weapon or any Epic Tier 2 weapon and apply it to your Heroic weapon.

  • Craft Skill Core items at the Skill Core NPC near the Altar of Calanthia Raid portal.

  • Heroic gear is leveled using the new craftable Heroic Growthstones as materials.

Ascended Arch Bosses: Queen Bellandir and Tevent

  • New upgraded difficulty versions of arch bosses Tevent and Queen Bellandir are now on the weekly schedule:

    • Ascended Arch Bosses take place at 4:00pm local server time on Tuesdays and Saturdays. The regular Queen Bellandir previously scheduled on Saturdays will now take place on Friday.

    • These new Arch Boss versions drop materials used to upgrade your existing Arch Boss weapons.

    • Tier 2 versions of the Tevent and Bellandir Arch Boss Weapons can now be crafted at any Weapon Crafter.

    • Crafting a Tier 2 Arch Boss weapon requires a Tier 1 Arch Boss weapon of the same type as crafting material.

    • For the consumed Tier 1 Arch Boss weapon, all traits will be reset and do not carry over to the Tier 2 weapon.

    • Tier 2 Arch Boss weapons have an enhanced weapon skill compared to their Tier 1 counterparts.

Arena Season 2

  • Arena Season 2 will end at the start of maintenance.

  • A month-long 6v6 Arena event season will begin after maintenance completes.

General

  • Markers: Ground markers can now be placed in the world to indicate tactical locations for your party.

    • By default, the Party Leader has access to place ground markers, but permissions may be managed through the Party UI.

  • Observer Mode expansion: Observer Mode can now be activated on PvE content such as Raids, Guild Raids, Dimensional Trials, and more. Manage Observer mode settings through the Party Management UI.

  • UI: Item Quickslots have been expanded from 4 slots to 8 slots.

  • Weapon Mastery: Tooltips for Weapon Mastery nodes now show the current stat benefit applied from the node, the value gained by the next level, and the value gained at max level.

  • Siege: Tax Delivery and Castle Siege now take place back-to-back on the same day instead of split across 2 weeks. The alternate week is now scheduled for Interserver Siege battles. These 2 events will cycle on a biweekly schedule.

  • Siege: Increased the duration of Fonsine's Blessing spawn protection provided when spawning during Sieges.

  • Chat: A variety of pre-set chat messages have been added that can be assigned to the ring menu for quick selection.

  • Chat: Message triggers may now be set to send pre-written chat messages based on activity triggers, like logins or party joins.

  • Riftstones and Boonstones: Increased the stat bonuses provided by Riftstone and Boonstone owner buffs.

  • Riftstones and Boonstones: Decreased the preparation timer at the start of Riftstone and Boonstone battles from 5 to 2 minutes.

  • Nebula Island: Adjusted the portal location for the boss portal in Seal Sanctuary to be further south.

  • Nebula Island: Grimturg: Adjusted some cases where Grimturg unexpectedly moved during mechanics.

  • Urstella Fields: Adjusted mob density in some areas.

  • Dungeons: 3 and 4-Star Dungeon reward chests may now be opened two times per run.

  • Nebula Island: The corruption debuff from Exodus will now be removed upon moving to a safe zone.

  • Contracts: Fixed an issue where interactable bottles in Talandre for the 'Collect Letters in a Bottle' contracts were only visible while within 5m of range.

  • Food: The buff granted by Enticing Cream Pudding now matches its description.

  • War Games: Players on different teams can no longer form a party together.

  • Crafting: Fixed an issue where the quantity button was inactive when supplementing materials from a selection chest during crafting.

  • Adventure Codex: Chapter 4: Fixed a case where the Rescue Operation codex could sometimes not be progressed.

  • Siege: Fixed a case where players could swap weapon types without incurring an intended ability cooldown.

Longbow

  • Flash Arrow: Increased area of effect by 2 meters. Now always attempts to hit, but the effect can be resisted by the target. On hit, applies a ranged evasion debuff to the target. Cooldown reduced from 60 seconds to 36 seconds, damage increased from 150% to 250%.

  • Brutal Arrow: Now increases damage by 40% against stunned or bound targets.

  • Weapon Mastery: Rhythmical Shooting: General weapon damage increases by 3~12 for 3 seconds each time you use different skills in sequence. Stacks up to 5 times.

  • Healing Touch: Added a fourth specialization that changes Healing Touch to a self-buff, adds a damage bonus when using longbow skills, but also reduces your movement speed for 4 seconds while active.

Spear

  • Phoenix Barrage: Reduced base damage. At Epic Lv 5, damage reduced from 91% of Base Damage +23, to 73% of Base Damage +19. Damage boost against monsters increased from 125% to 180%.

  • Explosive Fury: Reduced base damage. At Epic Lv 5, damage reduced from 360% of Base Damage +100, to 288% of Base Damage +80. Damage boost against monsters increased from 40% to 75%.

Dagger

  • Thundering Bomb: Reduced base damage. Reduced damage bonuses applied when striking from the back or when target is under Weaken effects. Damage boost against monsters increased from 15% to 40%.

  • Shadowy Escape: Fixed an issue where players would rarely teleport back to their casting point upon teleporting with Shadowy Escape.

Greatsword

  • Fixed an issue causing Death Blow and Guillotine Blade to be canceled if used a 2nd time while it's being charged.

Staff

  • Burning Smokescreen: Added a short cooldown before a 2nd Burning Smokescreen can be used to prevent it from accidentally being cast twice.

UI

  • Amitoi and Morphs: Tooltips on Amitoi and Morph icons now tell you if you've acquired that Amitoi or Morph.

  • Controller: Added the ability for controller shortcuts to be assigned to main menus such as Inventory.

  • Matchmaking: Changing zones or closing a menu no longer closes the 'Matchmaking Successful' popup resulting in it being declined.

  • Trials: Fixed an issue causing Trial-specific Dungeon UI elements to rarely not be displayed.

  • Codex: Fixed an issue causing the camera to occasionally get stuck when attempting to teleport to the Amitoi house while the camera pans during a cinematic scene.

  • PlayStation 5: The sun now appears without a black object in front of it.

Thanks for your support! We'll see you in Solisium.
author: news_parser

title: Raid: Altar of Calanthia - News
content:
Vanquish Calanthia before her dream becomes a nightmare for all of Solisium. The Altar of Calanthia, our first 12-player Raid, launches on June 26. Catch up with Tico, godspeed, and the team at NCSOFT for an in-depth look at everything you need to know about this new PvE activity.

Our Raid deep dive video covers design, character, narrative decisions, and more. After the video, read on for insight into future Calanthia appearances, plus details on difficulty. We’ve also shared concept art featuring Altar of Calanthia environments.

110 KB

The Command Marshal General of the Arkeum Legion

Fearsome bosses will attempt to stop you on your way to the altar. Upon your arrival, you’ll face the Command Marshal General of the Arkeum Legion.

Will players encounter Calanthia more in the future?

NCSOFT: In the existing Adventure Codex, the player grows alongside the Resistance after coming from Whisp Island. Throughout their journey, they meet various characters and solve incidents, becoming a central figure in that organization.

171 KB

Naturally, they also learn about key members, such as DaVinci and Janice Cartair, as well as important figures in Arkeum, like Reville Lupius, Calanthia, and Kazar. As part of the Adventure Codex and Bonus Codex updates releasing alongside the Raid, you will discover more not only about Calanthia but also about the stories of major Resistance figures, including Clay and Roen.

Experience the stories surrounding the Altar of Calanthia to find out whether you’ll encounter her more in the future.

Raid Difficulty

Find out what kind of experiences players can look forward to when challenging more difficult modes.

How will the difficulty of future raids be expanded?

NCSOFT: We are designing the difficulty to require both 'harmony' and 'growth'. It's not just about placing stronger enemies, but requiring organic party composition and individual role performance abilities.

154 KB

Additionally, progressing to higher difficulty levels will require learnings gained from lower-level raid experiences. Each raid is designed to provide players with meaningful triumphant moments.

Given that THRONE AND LIBERTY has many skilled PvE players, we have high expectations for their abilities. The hard difficulty mode of the Calanthia Raid is extremely challenging. Even top players will barely be able to clear it. However, as players regularly play it and acquire unique gear, the difficulty will gradually become more manageable.

For the future expert difficulty mode, players can expect an even more intense challenge. We can't wait to see players try it.

Altar of Calanthia Concept Art

See how the Raid went from concept to execution through the following images.

Fire and Ice Arena

177 KB

Calanthia

237 KB

Exterior

152 KB

Altar

149 KB

Corridor

173 KB

The Next Message

122 KB

Thanks for your support and good luck against Calanthia!
author: news_parser
